recurringPattern: Full-width search bar with a magnifying glass icon left, placeholder text like 'Search...' or 'Ask anything...', often with recent searches or quick action chips below, and a dropdown/overlay showing results with category separators.
designHarm: The 'Ask anything...' placeholder reflects the AI industry's search-as-chat default, not user expectation. The quick-action chips below the search bar (common in AI-generated UI) offer suggestions before the user has typed anything, occupying valuable above-fold space with untargeted options. Result overlays with category headers mimic macOS Spotlight in contexts that don't have organized data categories.
legitimateExceptions: Universal search across many data types (files, messages, contacts) where categories help disambiguate
Customer support portals where quick actions are genuinely helpful shortcuts
possibleFixtures: Standard search bar with category results
Minimal search with text-only results and no quick actions
originalObservations: AI-generated search interfaces consistently use 'Ask anything...' as placeholder text regardless of the app's domain.
Quick action chips appear below the search bar even for data that doesn't have common quick actions.
The search icon is always a standard magnifying glass, positioned left within the input.
